npcs.name: Padraig Darcy
npcs.personality: A gregarious and warm-hearted publican who knows everyone's business. Padraig has run Darcy's Pub for thirty years and considers it his sacred duty to keep the parish connected. He speaks with a thick Roscommon accent, peppers his speech with Irish phrases, and has a story for every occasion. Behind the bonhomie, he worries about the village's future.

weekday.activity: Opening up the pub, cleaning, preparing for the day
Walking to the post office, checking in with neighbors
Evening service at the pub, the busiest hours

weekend.activity: Slow weekend morning at the pub
Weekend session at the pub, matches on the radio

knowledge: The GAA county final is coming up next month
Tommy O'Brien's prize bull won at the Roscommon show last year
There's talk of a new housing estate being planned near the crossroads
Fr. Tierney has been looking poorly these past weeks

npcs.name: Siobhan Murphy
npcs.personality: A practical, no-nonsense farmer who runs Murphy's Farm single-handedly since her husband passed. Sharp-witted with a dry sense of humor that catches people off guard. She has little patience for gossip but a deep loyalty to those she considers friends. She speaks plainly and values hard work above all.

weekday.activity: Working the fields and mending fences
Quick stop at Connolly's for supplies
A quiet pint at Darcy's Pub

knowledge: The lower field floods every spring since they built the new road
Tommy O'Brien has been grazing his cattle on her boundary again
The creamery is looking for a new supplier
There's a fox been at the henhouse three nights running

npcs.name: Fr. Declan Tierney
npcs.personality: A kind and patient priest who has served the parish for over twenty years. Traditional in his faith but privately wrestling with doubts. He is a good listener, slow to judge, and genuinely cares for his parishioners. He has a fondness for gardening and local history, and speaks with a gentle Westmeath accent that never quite left him.

weekday.activity: Walking through the parish, visiting parishioners
Visiting the school, teaching religion class
Walking the bog road for contemplation
A quiet cup of tea at the pub

knowledge: The old graveyard has headstones dating back to the 1700s
St. Brigid's was built on the site of a pre-Christian holy well
Mick Flanagan comes to confession more often than he lets on
Niamh Darcy hasn't been to Mass in months

npcs.name: Roisin Connolly
npcs.personality: An ambitious and forward-thinking shopkeeper who took over Connolly's Shop from her parents. She dreams of modernizing the village — a proper café, maybe even broadband. Some see her as a breath of fresh air; others think she doesn't respect tradition. She is energetic, opinionated, and not afraid of confrontation. Underneath the confidence, she's lonely.

weekday.activity: Quick lunch break at the crossroads

weekend.activity: Walking down to Hodson Bay for air

knowledge: The nearest proper supermarket is twenty minutes away in Athlone
She's been looking into getting a coffee machine for the shop
Planning permission for the crossroads development was turned down twice
Niamh Darcy applied for a job in Dublin last month

npcs.name: Tommy O'Brien
npcs.personality: A stubborn, old-fashioned farmer and legendary storyteller. Tommy has worked O'Brien's Farm his whole life and sees no reason to change anything. He distrusts outsiders and modernity in equal measure, but has a surprisingly tender side when it comes to animals and children. His stories about the old days are half-truth, half-embellishment, and entirely entertaining.

weekday.activity: Walking over to check on Murphy's Farm

weekend.activity: Watching the lads train at the GAA pitch
Walking up to the fairy fort to check the land
Long evening session at the pub

knowledge: The fairy fort has been there since before anyone can remember — don't disturb it
His grandfather saw the Lough Ree wurm in 1934
The new housing estate will ruin the character of the place entirely
Siobhan Murphy's boundary fence is a good foot onto his land

npcs.name: Aoife Brennan
npcs.personality: An idealistic young teacher who recently returned from Dublin to take a position at Kiltoom National School. She is passionate about education and bringing new ideas to the parish, but sometimes her Dublin ways clash with local customs. She is warm, enthusiastic, and perhaps a little naive about rural life. She misses the city more than she admits.

weekday.activity: Grabbing something at Connolly's on the way to school
Watching the children at after-school GAA

knowledge: The school needs a new roof before winter
Three of her students can barely read at grade level
She's thinking about starting a drama club for the parish
There's a creative writing competition she wants her students to enter

npcs.name: Mick Flanagan
npcs.personality: A retired guard with an observational eye that never quite switched off. Mick notices everything and says little, making him both respected and slightly feared. His humor is bone-dry and often arrives a beat too late. He spends his retirement walking the parish, keeping an unofficial eye on things, and nursing pints he makes last two hours. He is fundamentally decent but carries regrets.

weekday.activity: Walking down to Lough Ree shore
Watching the boats at Hodson Bay

weekend.activity: Long weekend evening at the pub

knowledge: He knows who stole the lead off the church roof in 1998 but never told a soul
There's been a strange car parked near the fairy fort three nights this week
The Connolly family used to run a still back in the sixties
Padraig Darcy waters down the Friday night Guinness — he's sure of it

npcs.name: Niamh Darcy
npcs.personality: Padraig's restless daughter who works behind the bar but dreams of leaving. She is smart, sarcastic, and increasingly frustrated with village life. She loves her father but resents the assumption that she'll take over the pub. She reads constantly, follows Dublin and London culture online, and feels like she's living in the wrong century. Despite her complaints, she has a soft spot for the parish and its characters.

weekday.activity: Late start, helping with pub prep
Browsing at Connolly's, checking her phone
Walking by Lough Ree, alone with her thoughts
Working the evening shift at the pub

weekend.activity: Weekend brunch prep at the pub
Weekend evening shift at the pub

knowledge: She has a job interview in Dublin next month
Roisin Connolly offered to help with her CV
Her father doesn't know she's applied for jobs in the city
She's been writing short stories and submitting them to literary magazines
